reasoning: The baseline value on 2025-03-16 corresponds to the Wednesday, March 12, 2025 data release of 2417.0 for the Primary Credit facility. This level is relatively low compared to the moving average of the past two months (which hovered around 3000-3300) and significantly lower than Spring 2024 (where it reached 5000-7000). The FRED statistical model predicts a high likelihood (71%) of surpassing 2417 in the near term due to mean reversion and a slightly positive 6-month linear trend. Additionally, the Bank Term Funding Program (BTFP) one-year loans recently matured on March 11, 2025, which may drive some short-term replacement liquidity needs to the Discount Window. Consequently, the probability of exceeding 2417 for the nearest dates is elevated. However, looking at late Q3 and Q4 of 2024, borrowing dropped into the 1300-2000 range. Over the long horizons, as bank liquidity normalizes and pandemic/SVB crisis-era programs fully wind down, discount window usage historically reverts toward negligible amounts. Therefore, as time progresses, the likelihood of remaining above 2417 decreases and uncertainty grows. Probabilities thus scale downward toward 0.5 as the forecast horizon extends, tracking standard uncertainty growth.
